entries.title: DCOM unable to communicate with remote computer
entries.summary: DCOM failed to establish a connection to a remote computer, indicating a network, firewall, or RPC availability issue on the destination.
entries.details: Logged by the Microsoft-Windows-DistributedCOM provider in the System log when the local DCOM infrastructure cannot contact a remote host. Message format: 'DCOM was unable to communicate with the computer <computername> using any of the currently known IP addresses.' Key field: param1 (target computer name or IP). Uncommon in healthy environments; appears during DCOM-based lateral movement attempts when the target is unreachable or firewalled, and during normal operations when management tools reference decommissioned hosts.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Remote Services: Distributed Component Object Model

investigationPivots: Remote computer name/IP — is this a known management target or an anomalous pairing such as a workstation targeting a server?
Timestamp correlation with Security 4624/4625 and Sysmon 3 on both source and target hosts to determine if the connection was ultimately established
Source process — correlate with Sysmon EID 1 (process creation) to identify which process initiated the DCOM call
Frequency — a single 10009 is often benign; a burst from one source across multiple targets suggests automated lateral movement or C2 beaconing via DCOM
commonFalsePositives: SCCM, WSUS, or WMI-based monitoring tools targeting hosts that are offline or decommissioned
Group Policy processing attempting to contact domain controllers during network outages
Software licensing services and management agents that use DCOM for communication
Stale DNS records pointing DCOM calls to decommissioned IP addresses

keyFields.name: param1
keyFields.description: Target computer name or IP address that DCOM failed to reach; primary pivot for identifying lateral movement targets

detectionRules.title: DCOM Lateral Movement — 10009 Burst to Multiple Targets
detectionRules.rule:
Event
| where Source == "DCOM"
| where EventID == 10009
| parse EventData with * "<Data>" TargetHost "</Data>" *
| summarize TargetCount = dcount(TargetHost), Targets = make_set(TargetHost, 10) by Computer, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where TargetCount > 3
| project TimeGenerated, SourceComputer = Computer, TargetCount, Targets
detectionRules.notes: A single workstation generating 10009 against 3+ distinct hosts within 10 minutes is suspicious. Tune threshold based on environment size. Servers running SCCM or WMI-based monitoring may require exclusion.

detectionRules.title: DCOM Remote Communication Failure — Potential Lateral Movement Probe
detectionRules.rule:
title: DCOM Remote Communication Failure — Potential Lateral Movement Probe
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: system
detection:
    selection:
        Provider_Name: Microsoft-Windows-DistributedCOM
        EventID: 10009
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - SCCM or WMI monitoring tools targeting offline hosts
    - Stale DNS records
level: low
tags:
    - attack.lateral_movement
    - attack.t1021.003

entries.title: COM server failed to register with DCOM within timeout
entries.summary: A COM server (identified by CLSID) did not register with the DCOM service control manager within the required timeout period, indicating a crash, injection, or startup failure.
entries.details: Logged when DCOM launches a COM server process and that process fails to call CoRegisterClassObject within the default 120-second timeout. Message format: 'The server {<CLSID>} did not register with DCOM within the required timeout.' Key field: param1 (CLSID of the failing COM server). Resolve the CLSID via HKLM\SOFTWARE\Classes\CLSID\{GUID}. A hijacked COM server that does not implement the expected interface will fail to register, making this event a secondary indicator of COM hijacking activity.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Event Triggered Execution: Component Object Model Hijacking

investigationPivots: Resolve the CLSID in HKLM\SOFTWARE\Classes\CLSID\{GUID} — unknown CLSIDs not mapping to a known product are high-priority
Check HKCU\Software\Classes\CLSID\{GUID} for a user-space override (COM hijacking leaves traces here)
Correlate with Sysmon EID 1 (process creation) to see what process was launched for the CLSID and whether its image path is expected
Review Sysmon EID 12/13 (registry events) for recent changes to the CLSID registration keys
commonFalsePositives: Windows Update and servicing components during patch application or OS upgrades
Third-party software installers that register COM objects and restart the service
Antivirus or EDR products that intercept COM activation and cause registration delays
COM servers that crash during startup due to missing dependencies (DLL not found)

keyFields.name: param1
keyFields.description: CLSID of the COM server that failed to register; resolve to a friendly name via HKLM\SOFTWARE\Classes\CLSID

detectionRules.title: COM Server Registration Timeout — CLSID Baseline Deviation
detectionRules.rule:
Event
| where Source == "DCOM"
| where EventID == 10010
| parse EventData with * "<Data>" CLSID "</Data>" *
| summarize Count = count() by CLSID, Computer, bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| where Count == 1
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, CLSID, Count
detectionRules.notes: New or single-occurrence CLSIDs are more suspicious than recurring ones. Join with a CLSID allowlist derived from your software inventory for higher fidelity. Recurring 10010 for the same CLSID is usually a legitimate software issue.

detectionRules.title: COM Server Registration Timeout — Possible COM Hijacking
detectionRules.rule:
title: COM Server Registration Timeout — Possible COM Hijacking
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: system
detection:
    selection:
        Provider_Name: Microsoft-Windows-DistributedCOM
        EventID: 10010
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Windows servicing and update processes
    - Software installers registering COM objects
level: low
tags:
    - attack.persistence
    - attack.t1546.015

entries.title: DCOM application-specific permissions denied activation
entries.summary: A user or process attempted to activate or launch a COM server but was denied by the application-specific DCOM security permissions, generating a high-volume but security-relevant access denial record.
entries.details: Logged when a caller is denied Local or Remote Activation or Launch permission by the application-specific DCOM security descriptor. Message format: 'The application-specific permission settings do not grant Local [Activation|Launch] permission for the COM Server application with CLSID {<CLSID>} and APPID {<AppID>} to the user <domain\user> from address <IP>...' Key fields: param3 (CLSID), param4 (AppID), param8 (caller username), param12 (source IP). High volume in most environments due to Windows background COM activity; param12 distinguishes remote activation attempts from local noise.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Remote Services: Distributed Component Object Model

investigationPivots: Address field — a non-loopback, non-local IP address indicates a Remote activation attempt and is the highest-priority signal for DCOM lateral movement
User SID — a privileged account (e.g., SYSTEM, Administrator) should rarely be denied activation; a standard user or service account denied against a high-value CLSID warrants review

CLSID/AppID resolution — known attacker-favoured CLSIDs include MMC20.Application ({49B2791A-B1AE-4C90-9B8E-E860BA07F889}), ShellWindows ({9BA05972-F6A8-11CF-A442-00A0C90A8F39}), and ShellBrowserWindow ({C08AFD90-F2A1-11D1-8455-00A0C91F3880})
Frequency and process — bulk 10016 events from a single non-system process in a short window indicate automated lateral movement tooling rather than background Windows noise
commonFalsePositives: Windows system components (Runtime Broker, COM Surrogate) generating 10016 against themselves due to permission misconfiguration on default COM servers — extremely common and typically safe to suppress by CLSID
Microsoft Office components activating COM servers across integrity levels
Third-party security products that use COM for inter-process communication with insufficient DCOM permissions
IIS application pools activating COM servers under a restricted service identity

keyFields.name: param3
keyFields.description: CLSID of the COM server the caller attempted to activate; resolve in HKLM\SOFTWARE\Classes\CLSID

keyFields.name: param4
keyFields.description: AppID of the COM server; resolve in HKLM\SOFTWARE\Classes\AppID

keyFields.name: param8
keyFields.description: Caller username (domain\user); unexpected accounts (e.g., standard user on sensitive CLSID) are the primary pivot

keyFields.name: param12
keyFields.description: Source IP address of the activation request; a non-local IP indicates remote activation and lateral movement attempt

detectionRules.title: DCOM Remote Activation Denied — Lateral Movement Attempt
detectionRules.rule:
Event
| where Source == "DCOM"
| where EventID == 10016
| parse EventData with * "from address " SourceAddress " running" *
| where SourceAddress !startswith "127." and SourceAddress != "::1" and SourceAddress != "Local"
| parse EventData with * "user " CallerUser " SID" *
| parse EventData with * "CLSID " CLSID "}" *
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, CallerUser, SourceAddress, CLSID
detectionRules.notes: Filter on non-local source addresses to eliminate the bulk of Windows-generated noise. The resulting events represent remote activation attempts and should all be reviewed. Cross-reference CLSID against known lateral movement CLSIDs (MMC20.Application, ShellWindows, ShellBrowserWindow).

detectionRules.title: DCOM Remote Activation Denied — Possible Lateral Movement
detectionRules.rule:
title: DCOM Remote Activation Denied — Possible Lateral Movement
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: system
detection:
    selection:
        Provider_Name: Microsoft-Windows-DistributedCOM
        EventID: 10016
    filter_local:
        Message|contains:
            - 'from address 127.'
            - 'from address ::1'
            - 'from address Local'
    condition: selection and not filter_local
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate remote COM activation from management tools
level: medium
tags:
    - attack.lateral_movement
    - attack.t1021.003

entries.title: DCOM unable to communicate with computer via any known IP
entries.summary: DCOM exhausted all known IP addresses for a remote computer and could not establish a connection, indicating network-level blocking or host unreachability.
entries.details: Logged after DCOM exhausts all known IP addresses for a remote host without successfully establishing a connection. Message format: 'DCOM was unable to communicate with the computer <computername> using any of the currently known IP addresses.' Key field: param1 (target computer name). Similar to EID 10009 but explicitly indicates all resolved addresses were tried; both events often appear together. Isolated occurrences indicate offline or decommissioned hosts; a burst from one source across multiple targets in a short window suggests automated lateral movement.

mitreAttack.techniqueName: Remote Services: Distributed Component Object Model

investigationPivots: Target computer name — is this a high-value asset (domain controller, file server, database server)?
Burst pattern — more than 3 unique targets within 5 minutes from one source indicates automated scanning or lateral movement tooling
Correlation with Sysmon EID 3 on the source host to confirm outbound RPC connection attempts matching the 10028 targets
commonFalsePositives: Decommissioned servers still referenced in software configuration or DNS
Systems temporarily offline during maintenance windows
Domain-joined systems trying to reach domain controllers that are unreachable due to network partition

keyFields.name: param1
keyFields.description: Target computer name that DCOM could not reach after exhausting all known IP addresses

detectionRules.title: DCOM Scan — Multiple Unreachable Targets from Single Source
detectionRules.rule:
Event
| where Source == "DCOM"
| where EventID == 10028
| parse EventData with * "<Data>" TargetHost "</Data>" *
| summarize TargetCount = dcount(TargetHost), Targets = make_set(TargetHost, 20) by Computer, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where TargetCount > 3
| project TimeGenerated, SourceComputer = Computer, TargetCount, Targets
detectionRules.notes: Adjust the threshold and time window to your environment. In large organisations with extensive SCCM or WMI monitoring, raise the threshold. For workstations, even 2 unique targets in 5 minutes is unusual.

detectionRules.title: DCOM All IP Addresses Exhausted — Potential Lateral Movement Scan
detectionRules.rule:
title: DCOM All IP Addresses Exhausted — Potential Lateral Movement Scan
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: system
detection:
    selection:
        Provider_Name: Microsoft-Windows-DistributedCOM
        EventID: 10028
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Decommissioned or offline systems referenced in software configuration
    - Domain controllers temporarily unreachable
level: low
tags:
    - attack.lateral_movement
    - attack.t1021.003
    - attack.discovery
    - attack.t1018
